message:
test(embed/manifest): relax built-in-manifest download_size_bytes assertion for placeholder metadata

The `built_in_manifests_are_well_formed` test asserted
`manifest.download_size_bytes > 0` on every manifest. That was correct
when every shipped built-in had its download size baked into source,
but several newer manifests intentionally ship with:

    sha256 = PLACEHOLDER_VERIFY_AFTER_DOWNLOAD
    size   = 0

and defer the true sha256 + byte count verification to the first
download at runtime. That pattern exists so we don't ship stale
hashes (forcing a source edit every time a model host re-releases a
binary-identical artifact), but it meant the build-well-formed test
was failing for any placeholder-only manifest.

Fix: for each manifest, check whether EVERY file entry is flagged
`sha256 == PLACEHOLDER_VERIFY_AFTER_DOWNLOAD`. If so, skip the
non-zero-total assertion — those manifests legitimately carry
`download_size_bytes = 0` and will be populated post-download. If ANY
file has a real sha256, the original assertion still fires: a mixed
manifest with partial size metadata is still malformed.

The stronger "size sum invariant" assertion
(`download_size_bytes == Σ file.size`) is kept unchanged on the
placeholder-only manifests — it holds trivially (0 == 0) and would
still catch a manifest that accidentally set one but not the other.

No production code change; test-only assertion relaxation.
